elements.text:
Figure 26.5-1D. Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

Wind speed contour map of the United States showing basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings. The map displays wind speed contours with values ranging from 100(45) to 175(78) mph (m/s). The western states generally show lower wind speeds around 100-120 mph, while coastal areas and certain inland regions show higher values. The southeastern coastal areas and parts of the Great Plains show the highest wind speeds up to 175 mph. Hatched/shaded areas indicate regions with specific wind speed considerations. Contour lines are labeled with values like 105(47), 110(49), 115(51), 120(54), 130(58), 140(63), 150(67), 160(72), 170(76), and 175(78).
elements.caption: Figure 26.5-1D. Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

elements.text:
Figure 26.5-1D. Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

A wind speed contour map showing basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures across what appears to be the western United States. The map contains numerous contour lines with wind speed values in both mph and m/s (shown in parentheses). Values range from 100(45) in some interior areas up to 175(78) in coastal regions. The contours show higher wind speeds along coastal areas and lower speeds in interior regions. Hatched/shaded areas appear to indicate special wind regions or geographic features. The map includes state boundaries and shows detailed contour patterns with values like 105(47), 110(49), 115(51), 120(54), 130(58), 140(63), 150(67), 160(72), 170(76), and 175(78) distributed across different geographic regions.
elements.caption: Figure 26.5-1D. Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

elements.text: 1. Values are 3 s gust wind speeds in mi/h (m/s) at 33 ft (10 m) above ground for Exposure Category C.
2. Linear interpolation is permitted between contours. Point values are provided to aid with interpolation.
3. Islands, coastal areas, and land boundaries outside the last contour shall use the last wind speed contour.
1. Values are 3 s gust wind speeds in mi/h (m/s) at 33 ft (10 m) above ground for Exposure Category C.
4. Location-specific basic wind speeds shall be permitted to be determined using the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.
